properties.title: Tensions spike in final stretch of Hungarian election
properties.description: BUDAPEST — Tensions are escalating dramatically in the final stretch of Hungary’s election race as political rivals trade accusations of vote-buying in rural constituencies, illegal wiretapping and interference from Moscow and Kyiv. properties.description: RIGA, Latvia — Sindija Brakovska is 18 years old and dreams of becoming a hairstylist or a dance teacher. On this March morning, roughly two dozen girls between 16 and 18 are sitting in the classroom with Brakovska. Some of the girls look shy. Others sprawl with the studied boredom of teenagers everywhere. The room could be almost anywhere in Europe — if not for the rifles on the desks. They are clunky, black and weigh over 6 pounds. It’s the first parliamentary delegation to travel to China in eight years, the committee said. properties.title: Seafarers trapped in the Gulf are suffering — it’s time we paid attention
properties.description: Elisabeth Braw is a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, author of the award-winning “Goodbye Globalization” and a regular columnist for POLITICO. In the Red Sea, seafarers have endured more than two years of similar perils. These are not exceptions. As global confrontation intensifies, seafarers are suffering the consequences.
